message:
perf(indexer/tantivy): add rayon worker pool for lexical-rebuild prep + grow Tantivy add-batch defaults; expose CASS_TANTIVY_* knobs

Two coupled changes in the lexical-rebuild path that target the
prep + write halves of the same hot loop.  Both are env-tunable
so operators can dial them per host.

src/indexer/mod.rs — parallel rebuild prep
------------------------------------------
The rebuild pipeline previously did `prepare_lexical_rebuild_*`
work serially, even though each conversation can be normalized
independently.  On the typical bulk-rebuild workload the prep
step (HashMap lookup, ensure_cass_origin, message normalization,
counts) runs on every conversation in the batch, and at scale
this serial section was visible in flame graphs as the dominant
non-Tantivy cost.

This change extracts a `prepare_lexical_rebuild_conversation()`
helper so the per-conversation work has no shared mutable state,
adds a `prepare_lexical_rebuild_batch()` that fans out via
rayon's `par_iter()`, and gates the parallelism on a dedicated
worker pool created up front:

  - `lexical_rebuild_worker_parallelism()` reads
    `CASS_TANTIVY_REBUILD_WORKERS` (positive integer) or falls back
    to `available_parallelism()` clamped to 64.

  - `build_lexical_rebuild_worker_pool()` returns `Ok(None)` for
    parallelism ≤ 1 (so 1-core hosts skip the pool entirely) and
    otherwise builds a named pool (`cass-lexical-rebuild-{idx}`)
    so the threads are visible in `top`/`perf`.

  - `prepare_lexical_rebuild_batch()` `pool.install(prepare_jobs)`
    when a pool exists, falling back to the global rayon pool if
    none was provided.

`fetch_messages_for_lexical_rebuild_batch_chunked()` also gained
the worker_pool parameter and now actually honours
`sql_batch_fetch_conversation_limit` (the previous `let _ =
sql_batch_fetch_conversation_limit` was a TODO marker that left
the chunking logic dead).  When the number of conversations
exceeds the per-batch SQL fetch limit, it splits into chunks and
issues storage fetches in parallel through the same worker pool;
otherwise it stays on the existing single-shot fetch path.

src/search/tantivy.rs — bigger Tantivy add batches
--------------------------------------------------
Replaced the two compile-time constants with env-backed helpers:

  TANTIVY_ADD_BATCH_MAX_MESSAGES: 512 → CASS_TANTIVY_ADD_BATCH_MAX_MESSAGES
    default 4_096
  TANTIVY_ADD_BATCH_MAX_CHARS:    1 MiB → CASS_TANTIVY_ADD_BATCH_MAX_CHARS
    default 16 MiB

Tantivy's `Writer::run` (introduced in the recent
frankensearch-lexical change) amortises lock acquisitions across
the batch, so the prior 512-message / 1 MiB caps were leaving
performance on the table even on workstation-class machines.
Larger defaults match what bulk rebuilds actually want without
requiring tuning, and the env vars give operators a way to dial
back on memory-constrained hosts.

A new `CassDocContext` struct is extracted and a `cass_doc_context()`
factory function pulls out the per-conversation cass-origin
bookkeeping (source_id, origin_kind, origin_host, conversation_id,
workspace, source_path, title, started_at_fallback) so it can be
computed once per conversation and reused across each message's
document construction.  Cuts the number of Value→str→Value
hops per document substantially.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
